verses.1: By the rivers of Babylon, There we sat down and wept, When we remembered Zion.
verses.2: Upon the willows in the midst of it We hung our harps.
verses.3: For there our captors demanded of us songs, And our tormentors, jubilation, saying, “Sing for us one of the songs of Zion!”
verses.4: How can we sing the LORD’S song In a foreign land?
verses.5: If I forget you, Jerusalem, May my right hand forget its skill.
verses.6: May my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth If I do not remember you, If I do not exalt Jerusalem Above my chief joy.
verses.7: Remember, LORD, against the sons of Edom The day of Jerusalem, Those who said, “Lay it bare, lay it bare To its foundation!”
verses.8: Daughter of Babylon, you devastated one, Blessed will be one who repays you With the retribution with which you have repaid us.
verses.9: Blessed will be one who seizes and dashes your children Against the rock.
